generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only identifiers (features, properties) visible in the AADL model when constructing AGREE contracts.
Ensure AGREE contracts are placed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
When fixing type errors, align helper declaration types with the expected expression type (int, real, bool).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE uses `int`, `real`, and `bool` for scalar type declarations.
Use `eq` to define helper functions for complex expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL elements (features, properties) as part of a repair.
Avoid adding architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on correcting contract syntax or references.
